heading: List of certain telecommunications providers
label: 2679e List of certain telecommunications providers
notes: DefinitionsFor definitions of “Secretary”, “Department”, and “appropriate congressional committees” as used in this section, see , set out as a note under .section 5002 of Pub. L. 117–81section 263c of this title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–81, div. E, title LV, § 5501135 Stat. 2375Dec. 27, 2021“In this title [enacting this section and amending sections 4352 and 4354 of this title]:“(1).—Intelligence communityThe term ‘intelligence community’ has the meaning given such term in section 3(4) of the National Security Act of 1947 ().50 U.S.C. 3003(4)“(2).—Relevant congressional committeesThe term ‘relevant congressional committees’ means—“(A)the appropriate congressional committees;“(B)the Select Committee on Intelligence of the Senate; and“(C)the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence of the House of Representatives.”

children.heading: List of covered contractors
children.label: a List of covered contractors
children.text: Not later than 30 days after , the Secretary, in consultation with the Director of National Intelligence and other appropriate Federal agencies as determined jointly by the Secretary and the Director of National Intelligence, shall develop or maintain, as the case may be, and update as frequently as the Secretary determines appropriate, a list of covered contractors with respect to which the Department should seek to avoid entering into contracts. Not later than 30 days after the initial development of the list under this subsection, any update thereto, and annually thereafter for 5 years after such initial 30 day period, the Secretary shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees a copy of such list.December 27, 2021

the United States by, or on behalf of, any government, or persons associated with such government, listed as a cyber threat actor in the intelligence community’s 2017 assessment of worldwide threats to United States national security or any subsequent worldwide threat assessment of the intelligence community; or

individuals, including activists, journalists, opposition politicians, or other individuals for the purposes of suppressing dissent or intimidating critics, on behalf of a country included in the annual country reports on human rights practices of the Department for systematic acts of political repression, including arbitrary arrest or detention, torture, extrajudicial or politically motivated killing, or other gross violations of human rights.
